text:
, also spelt Rosé Thomas and known only as Rose in the 2009 anime, is a young girl living in the Eastern Amestrian town of Reole. A former Letoist adherent, Rose was a devout follower of the false prophet Cornello until the Elric brothers Edward and Alphonse exposed the priest's deception and opened her eyes to the truth. Due to the differences between the original manga storyline and the plot of the 2003 anime series, Rose's appearance and story relevance differ greatly between the two adaptations.

In Japanese media, her name is pronounced Rosé (ro-zay), like the wine, but spelt with no accent mark as Rose when romanised into English. In all English media aside from VIZ's English translation of the manga, her name is both spelt and pronounced like the flower, Rose.

Personality

In the Manga and 2009 anime, Rose is depicted as an overall kind and compassionate girl who is a staunch believer in the the teachings of Leto. She was a woman who had unwavering faith in the teachings of Father Cornello and would readily believe all his words, even believing that he would be able to bring her boyfriend back to life. This belief, possibly out of desperation, led her to be overall very defensive of the religion, reacting poorly when Ed challenged her beliefs and delighted when he feigned interest.

Upon realizing that the church of Leto was a fraud, Rose appeared to go under something of a crisis. Unable to accept that her boyfriend was truly gone, she was desperate for any solution to her problems, even wishing for the Philosopher's Stone to bring him back to life. However, after meeting with Ed and having time to reflect, Rose seems to have overcome her grief, choosing to believe in herself and putting her efforts into rebuilding the damaged Leore.

Manga and 2009 Anime

Rose first appears in Chapter 1 of the manga, where she greets the Elric brothers upon their fresh arrival in Reole. After the death of her boyfriend in an accident, Rose became enamored with Father Cornello's claims that the sun god Leto was capable of answering any prayer - even the resurrection of the dead - if said prayer were made by someone devout enough and became one of the priest's foremost believers. She, at first, scoffs at Edward's staunch disbelief in resurrection and a higher power, but after the Elrics help her confront Cornello and reveal that his "miracles" were the work of alchemy and his "great cause" a selfish pursuit of military power, she collapses spiritually. As Ed and Al leave the town, she demands angrily why they had to come and destroy the only hope she had left of reuniting with her lost love, to which Edward replies that she should stand on her own power and walk forward into a future she decides for herself.

Taking Ed's words to heart, Rose leads the effort to rebuild the town. There she meets Van Hohenheim, who enlists his services of help but does not reveal that he is the Elrics' father. Eventually Al and his allies arrived in the town following their escape from Fort Briggs. She makes a comment on how she is depressed that Ed is not with them, admitting that she has really missed him and that she wishes to see him again. This causes Winry to become slightly jealous, something Al catches and teases her with. Rose later helps Winry take a bath, and comments to her how she is thankful toward Ed and Al for opening her eyes to the false belief and got the town moving on their accord.

On The Promised Day, Rose was seen falling victim to Father's Nationwide Transmutation Circle along with the other Reole residents; but thanks to Hohenheim's fail safe, her soul was returned.

2003 anime

In the 2003 anime series, Rose's part begins similarly, but expands differently as the two plots diverge. Rose first appears in Episode 1 of the 2003 anime, she was a young girl living by herself in her hometown, inferring that her parents died sometime when she was a child. When she becomes older, she has a boyfriend who she cared for deeply, but then died in an accident. When Cornello arrives in their desert town claiming he is a prophet of the Sun God, Leto, he fills Rose with false hope telling her that he'll bring her dead boyfriend back. She then becomes a dedicated and faithful believer in the new church of Letoism, hoping that if she does good, it will increase her chances of bringing him back. When the Elric brothers arrive in town, they help her open her eyes to the fact that Cornello is a fraud using alchemy to do his miracles, lying to the town about being the son of Leto. Distraught by this, Rose doesn't know what to do, and Ed just promptly tells her that the only thing she can do is keep moving forward because she has two perfectly good legs to stand on.

In episode 14 it is revealed that the military was invading Reole where many of her people were slaughtered. Rose was eventually captured by the military and assaulted and raped. Due to the trauma of those experiences, Rose became mute, and some time later gave birth to a son, strongly implied by Scar to be the result of the rape. She was dubbed the Holy Mother by the citizens of Reole as part of Scar’s plan, silently serving as its figurehead while working with him and Lyra who spoke for her.

While escaping underground with everyone, they are attacked by Sloth and Wrath and try to flee. Out of worry, Rose decides to go back. In doing so, she manages to speak again, repeating the words Edward once told her in an attempt to snap him out of his shock at Sloth’s appearance, as she and her baby were being attacked by Wrath.

After they escape and share a brief moment of levity, Edward says it was their final goodbye, intending to never see her again but unknowingly leaving her with Dante, who they still believe is Lyra. Dante had planned to transfer her soul into Rose’s body, deceptively leading her to the underground city after escaping Reole. It is later shown that Dante had bounded Rose's mind, making her doll-like and obedient TV Animation Fullmetal Alchemist Characters Collection to her whims.

When Edward unexpectedly meets Rose again in the underground city, they dance together in a ballroom. Still under Dante’s control, Rose confesses her feelings to him, but Edward abruptly pushes her away and questions where Al is being kept. Dante, still masquerading as Lyra, beckons Rose over with her baby and calls Edward despicable for rejecting her. Rose then absently continues to watch on as the final events unfold.

After Edward is killed by Envy, the shock of his death breaks Dante’s control over Rose, leaving her horrified. By the end, after Al sacrificed himself to revive Ed and sent Envy to the Gate after failing to stop him, Rose had to tell Edward the grim news about his brother. After that, Edward tells Rose to leave the hidden city and to take Wrath with her. Not realizing his true plan, she told him similarly like he said at the beginning, that he would follow after them since he had two strong legs now due to his limbs being returned. This time, it would be their last meeting.

In the end when Al is returned as a human and Ed has disappeared to the other world, Rose and her baby are seen peacefully living with Al and the Rockbells in Resembool, her baby a few months older. She sees Al off with Winry at the train station after he decided to train under Izumi once more.

Conqueror of Shamballa

Rose appears in the movie, Conqueror of Shamballa. Before the events of the movie, Rose eventually goes back to Reole with her son in the desert, working with Alex Louis Armstrong to rebuild the city where they also built an orphanage together. Mizushima mentioned in the OCT. 2005 Animedia magazine that Rose and Armstrong built an orphanage together. When Al makes his first appearance in the movie, Rose is amazed because he looks just like Ed and because he has become so skilled with alchemy. She is later seen protecting the children and guiding the townspeople from danger. When the strange robots invading the town begin to float away, Al tries to grab them and hold them back, but Rose manages to pull him back saying, "Don't go, you'll disappear too!"

At the end of the movie, Rose is briefly seen outside in Resembool at the Rockbell residence along with Armstrong and three children, her baby from the series and the two orphans they took in, while Pinako watches on.

In the parallel world, Ed meets a Romani woman named Noah who looks very similar to Rose.

Dub vs Sub Differences

In the subbed version of the 2003 anime, both Elric brothers are shown to care for and think fondly for Rose, and Rose is shown to care for them as well. In the dubbed version of the anime, however, Edward and Rose's relationship was greatly emphasized while hers with Alphonse is diminished.

In Episode 1, while Alphonse watches Edward and Rose talk from behind the pillar, Alphonse originally comments, "Geez, Brother, you're just getting Rose-san upset at you." showing concern for Rose's feelings specifically and also respectfully using the -san honorific for her. In the dub, he doesn't seem to care for Rose specifically, instead just chastising Edward's cynicism: "Great, Ed. Push your total cynicism on someone else."

In Episode 2, when Rose goes to leave after bringing food to a chained up Edward, the dub version has Ed be more sympathetic to Rose: "I hope he's real Rose. I just don't want you to get disappointed." In the original subbed version, he simply asks "Was it really...your boyfriend?" In both cases, Rose is silent, leaving without giving a response.

In Episode 49, Dante, admiring Rose's body, states: "So pretty. It's no wonder why Ed and Al like you. They are his children." likely intending to highlight her obsession with Hohenheim by projecting it on both brothers (while not caring between the two) and Rose. The dub changes this line considerably, with Dante instead stating: "Exquisite. No wonder Edward loves you so much. He does, you know. And you love him too." Alphonse is never mentioned by Dante in the dubbed version. Later in the subbed version of the same episode, while Rose dances with Edward, Rose states, "I've been waiting for you, a long, long time. Ed, I..." to which Edward blushes, calling Rose by her name before abruptly pushing away from her to ask if she knows Alphonse's current location. While romantic feelings are hinted at, this is changed to be a very overt declaration in the dub: "I've been waiting... For you... For us... Edward. I love you." to which Edward simply responds, "You do?". Unlike in the subbed version, Edward doesn't ask about Alphonse, instead continuing the conversation: "Look Rose, I don't think you realize what you just said to me. We're just friends." In both versions, they are ultimately interrupted by the cry of Rose's baby and Dante's arrival.

In Episode 51 it’s in reverse, Alphonse in the dubbed version before transmuting himself says goodbye to Rose meanwhile in the subbed version he doesn’t acknowledge her at all.

Trivia

For some reason, Rose is only known by her first name in Brotherhood. Typically, when a character's full name is known, they are referred to by their full name when they appear on an eyecatch, however Rose's eyecatch only names her "Rose" with no surname given.
Rose is therefore the only character in Brotherhood who appears on a solo eyecatch and has a known full name which isn't used.
In Japanese, her name is pronounced Rosé (ro-zay) like Rosé wine, but is spelt with no accent mark like the flower, Rose. In the English dubs of both anime and related media, her name is both spelt and pronounced like the flower, Rose.
Rosé wine is a type of wine which incorporates some colouration from grape skins, but not enough to become red wine, leaving it a light pink colour instead. This makes her namesake similar to those of Martel, Dolcetto, and Roa, who are all named after various types and brands of alcohol.
In the 2003 anime series, Rose is portrayed as having dark skin, purple eyes and dark brown hair with pastel pink bangs. In the 2009 series however, she has pale skin, purple eyes, and black hair with dark red bangs. Both colour schemes were chosen before she was officially given one by Hiromu Arakawa, and as a result both turned out to be wrong. In actuality, Rose has light skin (like the 2009 anime) and bright pastel pink bangs (matching much closer to the 2003 anime). It would take until the game Fullmetal Alchemist Mobile for Rose to be depicted with manga-accurate colours.
While Rose was never shown coloured in the manga itself, her skin was not shaded like other dark-skinned characters are, meaning she was likely always intended to be light-skinned in the manga. The change to having dark skin was likely an intentional one by the creators of the 2003 anime, as that adaptation puts much more emphasis on political issues and atrocities committed by governments, including racial discrimination.
In an omake, Arakawa depicts Rose as a hardened warrior, in Joan of Arc-like style.
Rose, along with Cornello, are the only canonical characters to appear in the game Stray Rondo but not its sequel, Sonata of Memories. This is likely a consequence of Reole being the only canonical location from the previous game to be excluded from Sonata of Memories.
